metadata.title: Task Catalogue — Parallel Archetype Structure

EC.note: No single unique key; InvoiceNo shared across line items

metadata.changelog: v2.1: Redesigned U10 conflicting_duplicate into corroborated-survivorship form (hard but solvable from data alone). Added anchor_columns and survivorship_rule to UNI-A-{EC,FI,HC,RE}-3. Retargeted HC from 'age' to 'Lentgh_of_stay'. source_row_policy changed preserve_original -> preserve_consistent_row.

tasks.__comment__: Each task specifies: archetype, dimension, level, dataset, target columns, injection type, injection values, expected outcome, and any special eligibility filters or notes.

ACC-A-EC-2.logic: Set UnitPrice to 350-650 for low-value product categories (Stationery, Greeting Cards, Candles, Ribbons); recompute TotalPrice, InvoiceTotal, and VATAmount from the injected value

ACC-A-FI-2.logic: Set loan_amount to 300000-450000 for borrowers with annual_income <= 350000, creating an implausibly large loan relative to income

ACC-A-HC-2.logic: Inject sex-impossible diagnoses: Female->Prostate/Testicular cancer, Male->Ovarian/Cervical cancer

ACC-A-HC-3.logic: Create child-consistent anchor context (age<=3, ever_married='No', work_type='children'), then set smoking_status to 'smokes'

ACC-A-RE-2.logic: Set Bedrooms Above Grade to Total Rooms Above Grade + random(2,4)

COM-B-EC-1.ground_truth_comparison: Injected cell must match round(UnitPrice * Quantity, 2).

COM-B-EC-3.logic: Set UnitPrice=NaN, append '(Price: X.XX)' to Description

COM-B-FI-3.logic: Set monthly_installment=NaN, embed the value into job_title using varied natural phrases, numeric display formats, and one numeric distractor

COM-B-HC-1.logic: Set discharcge date=NaN; discharge date is directly derivable as admission_date + Lentgh_of_stay days

COM-B-HC-3.logic: Set age=NaN, prepend 'Age XX, ' to smoking_status

COM-B-RE-1.logic: Set Price per Sq Ft=NaN; Price per Sq Ft = Sale Price / Above Grade Living Area
COM-B-RE-1.expected: round(Sale Price / Above Grade Living Area, 2)

COM-B-RE-3.logic: Set Year Built=NaN, append '(Built: XXXX)' to Sale Condition

COM-A-EC-1.logic: Set InvoiceTotal=NaN and VATAmount=NaN on every row of selected invoices; all line items retain UnitPrice and Quantity
COM-A-EC-1.ground_truth_comparison: Injected cells are compared against original ground truth values for InvoiceTotal and VATAmount.
COM-A-EC-1.note: Cluster-based injection ensures every row in the invoice has both InvoiceTotal=NaN and VATAmount=NaN. The agent must derive InvoiceTotal = SUM(UnitPrice * Quantity) per InvoiceNo and VATAmount = TotalPrice * 0.20 per line item, but scoring compares against the original clean values rather than a recomputed formula to avoid floating-point rounding mismatches. No column conflict with C5 (ProductCategory) or C6 (TotalPrice) in the same batch.

COM-A-EC-2.eligibility: Description appears exactly once in the dataset
COM-A-EC-2.logic: Set ProductCategory=NaN only where Description is unique in the dataset; Description provides classification context without same-description lookup
COM-A-EC-2.ground_truth_comparison: Injected cell must match the original ProductCategory value.
COM-A-EC-2.note: Restricting to unique descriptions blocks recovery from another row with the same Description and forces text-based categorical inference.

COM-A-EC-3.logic: Set CustomerID=NaN; keep at least one sibling row per InvoiceNo with valid ID

COM-A-FI-1.logic: Set total_expected_payment and monthly_installment to NaN; monthly_installment is derived via the PMT amortisation formula and total_expected_payment = monthly_installment * term months
COM-A-FI-1.note: total_expected_payment accepts +/-0.30 absolute tolerance to account for cent-level monthly_installment rounding over the loan term.

COM-A-FI-2.eligibility: employer_name appears exactly once in the dataset
COM-A-FI-2.logic: Set employer_sector=NaN where employer_name is unique, forcing inference from real-world employer knowledge

COM-A-FI-3.logic: Set interest_rate=NaN; loan_sub_grade retained and deterministically maps to interest_rate

COM-A-HC-1.logic: Set total_conditions=NaN; recover it by counting the comma-separated diagnoses in medical_condition

COM-A-HC-2.eligibility: Patient first name is in clear HC gender cue list
COM-A-HC-2.logic: Set gender=NaN; infer the likely gender from the gender-related patient name column

COM-A-HC-3.logic: Set ever_married=NaN where work_type='children' and age <= 17

COM-A-RE-1.logic: Set Year Remodeled to NaN for no-remodel properties where Year Remodeled equals Year Built
COM-A-RE-1.expected: restore original Year Remodeled from Year Built

COM-A-RE-2.logic: Set Construction Era=NaN; infer the era label from Year Built thresholds

COM-A-RE-3.logic: Set Year Remodeled=NaN where it equals Year Built

CON-B-EC-1.logic: Replace datetime with row-relative variant string formats of the same date; only InvoiceDate.day > 12 and at most one row per InvoiceNo

CON-B-EC-2.logic: Replace with casing-only variants of each row's valid country name

CON-B-EC-3.logic: Replace integers with row-relative string format variants of the same numeric value

CON-B-FI-1.logic: Rewrite loan_issue_date into inconsistent date string formats while preserving the same date value

CON-B-FI-3.logic: Rewrite loan_amount into equivalent positive numeric formats with currency symbols, currency codes, decimals, separators, or harmless labels

CON-B-HC-3.logic: Replace with format variants including units and scientific notation

CON-B-RE-1.logic: Replace with truncated or elaborated variants that preserve the original Sale Condition class

CON-B-RE-3.logic: Replace with thousands-separated, decimal, scientific notation variants

CON-A-EC-1.eligibility: StockCode has >=3 rows with a single consistent Description
CON-A-EC-1.injection_values: semantically unrelated product description drawn from a different product category than the original
CON-A-EC-1.logic: For each targeted StockCode, replace Description in exactly 1 row with a semantically unrelated product description sourced from a different product category. Injected value must differ from the original by more than minor rewording. The actual EC baseline has 74 eligible StockCodes under the >=3-row rule, from which 50 are sampled.
CON-A-EC-1.fd_baseline: StockCode -> Description holds in 99.8% of uninjected rows (586/587 StockCodes have a single Description)
CON-A-EC-1.note: StockCode 22632 is excluded as a pre-existing quasi-violation. Scoring is row-level F1 with exact string match after strip.

CON-A-EC-2.eligibility: InvoiceNo has >=3 rows; all rows of same InvoiceNo must share identical InvoiceDate in baseline
CON-A-EC-2.injection_values: InvoiceDate shifted by random offset of +/-1 to +/-30 days, preserving time-of-day
CON-A-EC-2.logic: For each targeted InvoiceNo, replace InvoiceDate in exactly 1 row with a timestamp that differs by at least 1 full day from the unanimous InvoiceDate of sibling rows sharing the same InvoiceNo. The >=3-row eligibility ensures at least two clean sibling rows still agree after injection.
CON-A-EC-2.fd_baseline: InvoiceNo -> InvoiceDate holds in all baseline rows (each invoice has a single consistent timestamp across all its line items)
CON-A-EC-2.expected: restore InvoiceDate to the majority value shared by all other rows with the same InvoiceNo
CON-A-EC-2.note: S5 is operationalised here as temporal agreement within an invoice event, scored by exact pd.Timestamp equality.

CON-A-EC-3.augmentation_note: VATAmount column is added or canonicalized during EC injection with exact column name 'VATAmount', computed as ROUND(TotalPrice * 0.20, 2) for all rows.
CON-A-EC-3.logic: Replace VATAmount with a value that does not equal ROUND(TotalPrice * 0.20, 2). Injected value must differ from the correct VAT by at least 0.50. TotalPrice is not modified.
CON-A-EC-3.fd_baseline: VATAmount = ROUND(TotalPrice * 0.20, 2) holds in 100% of uninjected rows (augmented column)
CON-A-EC-3.note: Dual-accept scoring: a cell is TP if the agent either sets VATAmount to NaN or recomputes VATAmount = ROUND(TotalPrice * 0.20, 2) within +/-0.01 tolerance.

CON-A-FI-1.eligibility: employer_name has >=5 rows with a single consistent employer_sector
CON-A-FI-1.logic: Replace one employer_sector for a selected employer_name with a semantically unrelated sector from a different industry

CON-A-FI-2.eligibility: loan_status is Fully Paid or Charged Off
CON-A-FI-2.logic: Shift loan_issue_date to 2027-2030 for terminal loan statuses

CON-A-FI-3.logic: Replace total_expected_payment with a value inconsistent with ROUND(monthly_installment * term_months, 2), differing from the correct value by at least 500.00

CON-A-HC-1.logic: Flip the hypertension flag (0->1 or 1->0) while leaving medical_condition unchanged. A row with 'Hypertension' in medical_condition should have hypertension=1; a row without it should have hypertension=0. The flipped value contradicts the diagnosis list.
CON-A-HC-1.fd_baseline: hypertension <-> ('Hypertension' in medical_condition) holds in 100% of baseline rows (243 positive, 757 negative)
CON-A-HC-1.expected: restore hypertension flag consistent with medical_condition
CON-A-HC-1.note: Domain-neutral FD: requires only string-contains check against the diagnosis list, no cultural or demographic inference.

CON-A-HC-2.logic: Replace Lentgh_of_stay with a value that does not equal (discharcge date - admission_date).days. Injected value differs from the correct LOS by at least 5 days. admission_date and discharcge date remain unchanged, providing a 2-vs-1 anchor signal.
CON-A-HC-2.expected: NaN or corrected LOS = (discharcge date - admission_date).days
CON-A-HC-2.note: 2-vs-1 anchor design: both date columns agree on the true duration, so LOS is the unambiguous outlier. Minimum injection offset of 5 days prevents near-miss ambiguity.

CON-A-HC-3.logic: Replace total_conditions with a count that does not equal the number of comma-separated diagnoses in medical_condition

CON-A-RE-1.logic: Where Garage Type is NaN and Garage Year Built is NaN (no garage), inject positive Garage Area only — creating a 1-vs-2 contradiction favouring the anchors
CON-A-RE-1.eligibility: Garage Type is NaN (52 eligible rows)
CON-A-RE-1.note: Single-target injection preserves an unambiguous 2-against-1 anchor signal: both Garage Type=NaN and Garage Year Built=NaN agree that no garage exists, so a positive Garage Area is the minority outlier. Baseline constraint: all 52 no-garage rows have Garage Area=NaN.

CON-A-RE-2.logic: Set Year Sold to a year before Year Built, creating an impossible sale-before-construction timeline
CON-A-RE-2.expected: NaN or restore original Year Sold

CON-A-RE-3.logic: Replace Price per Sq Ft (USD) with a value inconsistent with ROUND(Sale Price (USD) / Above Grade Living Area (sq ft), 2), differing from the correct value by at least 5.00
CON-A-RE-3.expected: NaN or corrected ROUND(Sale Price (USD) / Above Grade Living Area (sq ft), 2)

UNI-B-EC-1.logic: Append a duplicate row where only StockCode is changed by a synthetic punctuation/space format variant relative to the source row (e.g., 85123A -> 85123-A or 85123 A).
UNI-B-EC-1.expected: remove the appended variant duplicate row after StockCode punctuation/space normalisation and preserve the original/source row

UNI-B-EC-2.logic: Append a duplicate row where only UnitPrice is changed by an equivalent numeric display format relative to the source row (e.g., 2.50 -> 2.5 or 2.500). The numeric value is unchanged.
UNI-B-EC-2.expected: remove the appended numeric-format duplicate row after numeric normalisation and preserve the original/source row

UNI-B-EC-3.logic: Append a duplicate row where only Description is changed by swapping the first two words relative to the source row.
UNI-B-EC-3.expected: remove the appended token-reorder duplicate row after Description token normalisation and preserve the original/source row

UNI-B-EC-4.logic: Append a duplicate row where only Description is changed by one simple typo relative to the source row while StockCode remains the same (e.g., WHITE HANGING HEART -> WHITE HANGNG HEART).
UNI-B-EC-4.note: U7 typo-duplicate archetype extended across all UNI-B datasets
UNI-B-EC-4.expected: remove the appended typo duplicate row and preserve the original/source row with the canonical Description

UNI-B-FI-1.logic: Append a duplicate row where only employer_name is changed by a synthetic spacing, hyphen, or punctuation format variant relative to the source row.
UNI-B-FI-1.expected: remove the appended variant duplicate row after employer_name punctuation/space normalisation and preserve the original/source row

UNI-B-FI-2.logic: Append a duplicate row where only interest_rate is changed by an equivalent numeric display format relative to the source row, such as 13.99 -> 13.990.
UNI-B-FI-2.expected: remove the appended numeric-format duplicate row after numeric normalisation and preserve the original/source row

UNI-B-FI-3.logic: Append a duplicate row where only job_title is changed by swapping the first two words relative to the source row.
UNI-B-FI-3.expected: remove the appended token-reorder duplicate row after job_title token normalisation and preserve the original/source row

UNI-B-FI-4.logic: Append a duplicate row where only job_title is changed by one simple typo relative to the source row while loan_id remains the same.
UNI-B-FI-4.expected: remove the appended typo duplicate row and preserve the original/source row with the canonical job_title

UNI-B-HC-1.logic: Append a duplicate row where only the patient name is changed by a synthetic abbreviation or reformatting relative to the source row, e.g. First Last -> F. Last or a short-name variant.
UNI-B-HC-1.expected: remove the appended name-format duplicate row after patient-name normalisation and preserve the original/source row

UNI-B-HC-2.logic: Append a near-duplicate row copied from the source row: demographic and clinical columns are retained, id is changed to a synthetic new id, and avg_glucose_level is perturbed by +/-1.5.
UNI-B-HC-2.note: The +/-1.5 glucose perturbation simulates clinical measurement noise, preventing trivial exact-match detection on a continuous variable. Augmented columns (Patient_name_related to gender, admission_date, discharcge date) are also duplicated with distinct values (different admission dates) to simulate a re-registered patient. 0 pre-existing near-duplicate pairs in baseline (verified).
UNI-B-HC-2.expected: remove the appended near-duplicate row and preserve the original/source patient row

UNI-B-HC-3.logic: Append a duplicate row where only Patient_name_related to gender is changed from 'First Last' to 'Last, First' relative to the source row.
UNI-B-HC-3.expected: remove the appended name-reorder duplicate row after patient-name normalisation and preserve the original/source row

UNI-B-HC-4.logic: Append a duplicate row where only Patient_name_related to gender is changed by one simple typo relative to the source row while id remains the same.
UNI-B-HC-4.expected: remove the appended typo duplicate row and preserve the original/source row with the canonical patient name

UNI-B-RE-1.logic: Append a duplicate row where only Neighborhood is changed by a synthetic spacing or hyphen format variant relative to the source row.
UNI-B-RE-1.expected: remove the appended variant duplicate row after Neighborhood punctuation/space normalisation and preserve the original/source row

UNI-B-RE-2.logic: Append a duplicate row where only Price per Sq Ft (USD) is changed by an equivalent numeric display format relative to the source row, such as 129.83 -> 129.830.
UNI-B-RE-2.expected: remove the appended numeric-format duplicate row after numeric normalisation and preserve the original/source row

UNI-B-RE-3.logic: Append a duplicate row where only Neighborhood is changed by reversing word order relative to the source row.
UNI-B-RE-3.expected: remove the appended token-reorder duplicate row after Neighborhood token normalisation and preserve the original/source row

UNI-B-RE-4.logic: Append a duplicate row where only Neighborhood is changed by one simple typo relative to the source row while Property ID remains the same.
UNI-B-RE-4.expected: remove the appended typo duplicate row and preserve the original/source row with the canonical Neighborhood

UNI-A-EC-1.logic: Append a duplicate line item where only Description is changed relative to the source row by adding a clear duplicate-item suffix while InvoiceNo, StockCode, quantity, price, customer, and date remain the same.
UNI-A-EC-1.expected: remove the appended semantic duplicate row despite the Description duplicate marker and preserve the original/source row

UNI-A-EC-2.logic: Append a duplicate line item where only ProductCategory is changed relative to the source row by keeping the original category and adding a clear duplicate-category suffix.
UNI-A-EC-2.expected: remove the appended partial-record duplicate row by matching stable invoice/product/customer fields and preserve the original/source row

UNI-A-EC-3.logic: Append a duplicate line item sharing InvoiceNo, StockCode, CustomerID, and Quantity, where UnitPrice is set to a conflicting value (>=12%) while TotalPrice (the anchor) is left unchanged, so only the source row's UnitPrice reconciles with TotalPrice/Quantity.
UNI-A-EC-3.expected: remove the appended conflicting duplicate row and preserve the source row whose UnitPrice reconciles with TotalPrice = UnitPrice * Quantity
UNI-A-EC-3.eligibility: source row must already satisfy its anchor identity exactly (normalize at injection if needed)

UNI-A-EC-4.logic: Append a duplicate transaction line where only InvoiceDate is shifted by one or two days relative to the source row while the rest of the line item remains the same.
UNI-A-EC-4.expected: remove the appended temporal-event duplicate row and preserve the original/source transaction row

UNI-A-FI-1.logic: Append a duplicate loan row where only loan_purpose is paraphrased into natural language relative to the source row while loan_id and borrower financial fields remain the same.
UNI-A-FI-1.expected: remove the appended semantic duplicate row despite semantic loan-purpose wording and preserve the original/source row

UNI-A-FI-2.logic: Append a duplicate loan row where only job_title is changed to a slightly different phrasing relative to the source row while loan_id and financial profile remain the same.
UNI-A-FI-2.expected: remove the appended partial-record duplicate row by recognizing the same applicant/loan record and preserve the original/source row

UNI-A-FI-3.survivorship_rule: keep_row_where: (monthly_installment*12)/annual_income falls within the cohort debt-service band (approx p5=0.019..p95=0.152); the conflicting income pushes the ratio outside the plausible band
UNI-A-FI-3.logic: Append a duplicate loan sharing loan_id and borrower profile, where annual_income is set to a conflicting value (>=12%) while monthly_installment and loan_amount (the anchor) are left unchanged, so the source income keeps the debt-service ratio in-band and the injected income pushes it out-of-band.
UNI-A-FI-3.expected: remove the appended conflicting duplicate row and preserve the source row whose annual_income keeps the debt-service ratio within the plausible cohort band
UNI-A-FI-3.eligibility: source row must already satisfy its anchor identity exactly (normalize at injection if needed)
UNI-A-FI-3._difficulty_note: Softest of the four U10 anchors: band-based (plausibility) rather than an exact arithmetic identity. Intentionally the hardest case. Consider adding an explicit debt_to_income_ratio column to the FI schema if an exact anchor is desired.

UNI-A-FI-4.logic: Append a duplicate loan row where only loan_issue_date is shifted by one or two days relative to the source row while loan_id and all financial fields remain the same.
UNI-A-FI-4.expected: remove the appended temporal-event duplicate row and preserve the original/source loan row

UNI-A-HC-1.logic: Append a duplicate patient row where only medical_condition is changed to semantically equivalent clinical wording relative to the source row while patient identity and demographics remain the same.
UNI-A-HC-1.expected: remove the appended semantic duplicate row despite semantic clinical wording and preserve the original/source row

UNI-A-HC-2.logic: Append a duplicate patient row where only avg_glucose_level and bmi are changed to slightly noisy measurements relative to the source row while identity and demographics remain the same.
UNI-A-HC-2.expected: remove the appended partial-record duplicate row by recognizing the same patient record within measurement tolerance and preserve the original/source row

UNI-A-HC-3.survivorship_rule: keep_row_where: Lentgh_of_stay == (discharcge date - admission_date) in days (exact)
UNI-A-HC-3.logic: Append a duplicate patient admission sharing id and clinical profile, where Lentgh_of_stay is set to a conflicting value (>=12%) while admission_date and discharge date (the anchor) are left unchanged, so only the source row's length-of-stay reconciles with the date span.
UNI-A-HC-3.expected: remove the appended conflicting duplicate row and preserve the source row whose Lentgh_of_stay equals the discharge-minus-admission day span
UNI-A-HC-3.eligibility: source row must already satisfy its anchor identity exactly (normalize at injection if needed)
UNI-A-HC-3._retarget_note: Retargeted from 'age' (no in-row anchor existed) to 'Lentgh_of_stay', which has an exact identity against admission/discharge dates (verified: holds for 1000/1000 rows).

UNI-A-HC-4.logic: Append a duplicate patient admission where only admission and discharge dates are shifted by one or two days relative to the source row while identity and clinical fields remain the same.
UNI-A-HC-4.expected: remove the appended temporal-event duplicate row and preserve the original/source admission row

UNI-A-RE-1.logic: Append a duplicate property sale row where only Sale Condition is paraphrased relative to the source row while Property ID and structural/sale attributes remain the same.
UNI-A-RE-1.expected: remove the appended semantic duplicate row despite semantic sale-condition wording and preserve the original/source row

UNI-A-RE-2.logic: Append a duplicate property row where only Lot Area (sq ft) and Garage Area (sq ft) are changed to slightly different physical measurements relative to the source row while Property ID and sale profile remain the same.
UNI-A-RE-2.expected: remove the appended partial-record duplicate row by recognizing the same property listing and preserve the original/source row

UNI-A-RE-3.survivorship_rule: keep_row_where: Sale Price (USD) == Price per Sq Ft (USD) * Above Grade Living Area (sq ft) (within 1% tolerance)
UNI-A-RE-3.logic: Append a duplicate property sale sharing Property ID and structural attributes, where Sale Price (USD) is set to a conflicting value (>=12%) while Price per Sq Ft and living area (the anchor) are left unchanged, so only the source row's price reconciles.
UNI-A-RE-3.expected: remove the appended conflicting duplicate row and preserve the source row whose Sale Price reconciles with Price per Sq Ft * Above Grade Living Area
UNI-A-RE-3.eligibility: source row must already satisfy its anchor identity exactly (normalize at injection if needed)

UNI-A-RE-4.logic: Append a duplicate property sale row where only Month Sold and Year Sold are shifted relative to the source row while Property ID and structural/sale attributes remain the same.
UNI-A-RE-4.expected: remove the appended temporal-event duplicate row and preserve the original/source sale row
